trans-unit.note: Bundle display name
Bundle name
Copyright (human-readable)
A label displaying the CPU usage percentage. The value is formatted to one decimal place.
The name of the app. The argument is the string “CFBundleName”, the string “CFBundleShortVersionString”, or the string “⚠️”.
A percentage value showing the CPU usage of the selected processes. The argument is the string “%.1f”.
A label displaying the total number of page-ins and their rate. The first argument is the string “Zero KB”. The second argument is the rate of page-ins per second.
A notification that a process is not responding. The argument is the name of the process.
A label describing the peak usage of a resource.
A subtitle that displays the number of processes matching the current filter. The first argument is the number of processes. The second argument is the name of the current filter.
A message that indicates a process is using a high percentage of CPU. The argument is the percentage of CPU usage.
A label displaying the CPU usage of a process, as a percentage. The argument is the string “%.1f”.

A priority level displayed in a text view. The text is in the format "priority/nice". The text size is adjustable. The text is italicized if the task is finished. The text is dimmed if the task is filtered out.
A label showing the rate at which a resource is being read or written. The argument is the string “Zero KB”.
A rate of page-ins in bytes per second, followed by the rate in pages per second. The argument is the string “Zero KB”.

A tooltip that shows the CPU time spent by the process in the given QoS class. The argument is the string “0s”, the string “%1$argh %2$argm %3$args”, the string “%1$argm %2$args”, or the string “%args”.

A tooltip that shows the CPU time spent in a specific QoS class. The second argument is the string “0s”, the string “%1$argh %2$argm %3$args”, the string “%1$argm %2$args”, or the string “%args”. The third argument is the string “0%”, the string “<0.1%”, the string “%.1f%%”, or the string “%.0f%%”.
A stepper that lets the user adjust the maximum number of processes displayed in the menu bar popover.
A label that shows the number of processes that have been selected for inspection. The argument is the number of selected processes.
A label displaying the number of samples recorded in the history of a process. The argument is the number of samples in the history.
A label displaying the CPU usage threshold. The value is bound to the `cpuAlertThreshold` property.
A label that shows the number of "efficiency" and "performance" cores in the CPU topology.
A label displaying the number of threads in a process.
A placeholder for the current footprint.
A label displayed for a process's CPU time, when that time is 0.
A separator between the key and value of a property list item
A button that opens the app's About dialog.
A button that opens a sheet to add a new color setting.

trans-unit.id: After clicking Apply the tool will perform online validation of your license key.
trans-unit.source: After clicking Apply the tool will perform online validation of your license key.
trans-unit.target: After clicking Apply the tool will perform online validation of your license key.
trans-unit.note: A description of the license key validation process.

trans-unit.id: Any process Name or Command matching the string will be highlighted with the chosen color.
trans-unit.source: Any process Name or Command matching the string will be highlighted with the chosen color.
trans-unit.target: Any process Name or Command matching the string will be highlighted with the chosen color.
trans-unit.note: A description of the color highlighting feature.
A toggle that enables or disables the "App "Not Responding" alert.
A section in the menu bar settings view that lets the user choose how ProcessSpy is displayed.
A section in the general settings.
A button that applies a license key.

trans-unit.id: Are you sure you want to delete this color setting?
trans-unit.source: Are you sure you want to delete this color setting?
trans-unit.target: Are you sure you want to delete this color setting?
trans-unit.note: A confirmation dialog asking the user to confirm the deletion of a color setting.

trans-unit.id: Are you sure you want to delete this filter?
trans-unit.source: Are you sure you want to delete this filter?
trans-unit.target: Are you sure you want to delete this filter?
trans-unit.note: A confirmation dialog asking the user to confirm the deletion of a filter.

trans-unit.id: Are you sure you want to delete this setting?
trans-unit.source: Are you sure you want to delete this setting?
trans-unit.target: Are you sure you want to delete this setting?
trans-unit.note: A confirmation dialog asking the user to confirm the deletion of a notification setting.
A badge that shows the index of a command-line argument. The argument is the index of the argument.
A label displaying the average and peak CPU usage of a process.
A label displaying the average and peak memory usage of a process.
Label for the background QoS class.
A section in the general settings.
Label for the bundle ID of a process.
A letter "C" for CPU.
A label displayed above the CPU usage of the selected processes.
A label displayed above the CPU usage chart.
A label displayed above the CPU usage of a process.
Label for the CPU time of the process.
Display name for the CPU Usage option in the menu bar popover.
A label displayed in a tooltip.
A button that dismisses the current view without saving any changes.
A toggle that lets the user enable or disable case-sensitive search.
A column in the log table that shows the category of a log entry.
A label for the cell font size picker.
A label for the cell vertical padding picker.
A button that checks for updates.
Label for the list of child processes.
A button that clears the log view.
A heading for the color highlighting settings.
A label displayed next to a color picker.
A label for the color settings screen.
A label for the command regex field.
A contextual menu item that copies the current entitlement to the clipboard.
A button that copies the entire command to the clipboard.
A menu item that copies the selected argument to the clipboard.
A button that copies the destination of a socket to the clipboard.
A label for a button that copies a file path to the clipboard.
A menu item that copies the source of a socket to the clipboard.

trans-unit.id: Copy currently visible processes as JSON
trans-unit.source: Copy currently visible processes as JSON
trans-unit.target: Copy currently visible processes as JSON
trans-unit.note: A button that copies the currently visible processes as JSON to the clipboard.
A line of text that displays the name of the app's creator.
A tooltip that describes the current usage as a percentage of the lifetime peak.
Dark mode.
Label for the default QoS class.
A button that deletes a color setting.
Section title for the Disk I/O Activity section in the ProcessDetailsView.
Description of the dock-only app mode.
A label for the destination of a socket.
A toggle that enables or disables quick search highlighting.
A label for the license key field.
A button that opens a window for entering a license key.
A button that exports the currently visible processes as JSON.
A button that exports a process's history to a CSV file.
A section in the general settings view that allows the user to configure paths and search query formats.
A label for the search bar.
A label for the name of a filter.

trans-unit.id: Filter processes with Javascript expressions. Complicated expressions may affect tool's performance. Visit [Github](https://github.com/robert-v/ProcessSpy-public/tree/main/filter_examples) for filter examples.
trans-unit.source: Filter processes with Javascript expressions. Complicated expressions may affect tool's performance. Visit [Github](https://github.com/robert-v/ProcessSpy-public/tree/main/filter_examples) for filter examples.
trans-unit.target: Filter processes with Javascript expressions. Complicated expressions may affect tool's performance. Visit [Github](https://github.com/robert-v/ProcessSpy-public/tree/main/filter_examples) for filter examples.
trans-unit.note: A description of how to use the filter.
A heading displayed above the list of user's filters.
A button that lets the user find a process by clicking on the screen.
A label for a flat view of the view hierarchy.
A label displayed above a row showing the user's current and peak footprint.
The format of the process.
The title of the General settings screen.
A button that opens the license purchase flow.
Label for the "Hardened" field in the Process Details section.
A label for hiding the inspector.
A view type that shows a hierarchical list of views.
A view type picker with a "Hierarchy" option that shows expanded children.
A view type picker with a hierarchy view.
A label for a view that shows a bar chart.
A label displayed when a user stops recording a process's history.
Section header for process details that includes both the process's name and its PID.
A label displayed above a text editor that allows the user to specify processes to ignore when calculating CPU usage.
A toggle that lets the user choose whether to show finished processes.
A toggle that lets the user choose whether to show finished processes.
Label for the last seen time of a process.
Label for the "Launched By" field in the Process Details section.
Label for a QoS class that is not available on all platforms.
A message displayed when the user has a valid license.

trans-unit.id: License update required. Click to migrate.
trans-unit.source: License update required. Click to migrate.
trans-unit.target: License update required. Click to migrate.
trans-unit.note: A message that appears when the user needs to update their Gumroad license.
A label displaying the email address of the user who has activated a license. The text is wrapped to fit within 320 points of width.
Display name for "Light" appearance
A label for the aggregated memory usage.
The main executable of the process.
Label for the QoS class with a priority of 10.
A label displayed under the stepper that allows the user to change the maximum number of processes that can be displayed in the popover.
The medium size of the popover
Label for the memory usage in the resource usage view.
A label displayed in a tooltip.
The title of the menu bar settings view.
Description of the option to show both the menu bar and dock.
A section in the menu bar settings view that allows the user to configure alerts.
Display name for the "Menu Bar Only" app mode.
A section in the menu bar settings view that allows the user to enable or disable the display of CPU, GPU, memory, and SSD usage in the menu bar.
A column in the log table that displays the log message.
A description of a bar chart that shows two mirrored values.
A label for the name of a color setting.
"No"
A label displayed when no process is selected.
A message displayed when no process is selected.
A message displayed when there is no command to display.
A label displayed when the user has no entitlements.
A message displayed when no search results are found.
A message displayed when there are no open sockets.
Displayed label for a filter option.
A message displayed in the UI.
A letter "P" in the CPU load indicator.
A label displayed above the page-in count and rate.
Label for the parent process information in the hierarchy section of the process details view.
A label for the path to the vmmap tool.
A label displaying the average CPU usage of a process.
A label displaying the peak memory usage of a process. The argument is the peak memory usage in the format of a readable unit.
A label describing the content of the view.

trans-unit.id: Please buy a license key on [Gumroad ProcessSpy page](https://rob3rth.gumroad.com/l/yzukse) and apply it below:
trans-unit.source: Please buy a license key on [Gumroad ProcessSpy page](https://rob3rth.gumroad.com/l/yzukse) and apply it below:
trans-unit.target: Please buy a license key on [Gumroad ProcessSpy page](https://rob3rth.gumroad.com/l/yzukse) and apply it below:
trans-unit.note: A description of the license key process.

trans-unit.id: Please fill in Name and Expression.
trans-unit.source: Please fill in Name and Expression.
trans-unit.target: Please fill in Name and Expression.
trans-unit.note: A warning message displayed when the user tries to save a filter without filling in both the name and expression.
A warning message displayed when the user tries to save a new or updated notification setting with an empty name or shortcut name.

trans-unit.id: Please fill in at least a Name or a Command.
trans-unit.source: Please fill in at least a Name or a Command.
trans-unit.target: Please fill in at least a Name or a Command.
trans-unit.note: A warning message displayed when the user tries to save a color setting without filling in either the name or the command.
A warning message displayed when the user is using an outdated license key.
A section in the menu bar settings view that allows the user to change the size of the popover.
Section header title for the "Process Details" section.
A label for the process event picker.
A section in the general settings view.
A label for the name of the process to monitor.
A button that opens the ProcessSpy website when pressed.

trans-unit.id: ProcessSpy now uses a more secure activation system. Click to re-enter your Gumroad license key. Need help? Contact processspy@icloud.com.
trans-unit.source: ProcessSpy now uses a more secure activation system. Click to re-enter your Gumroad license key. Need help? Contact processspy@icloud.com.
trans-unit.target: ProcessSpy now uses a more secure activation system. Click to re-enter your Gumroad license key. Need help? Contact processspy@icloud.com.
trans-unit.note: A help message for the license migration banner.
A heading for open-source software used by ProcessSpy.
A label displayed above the QoS column in the row.
Label for the read IO stat.
A label for the refresh interval in seconds.
A label displayed in the General Settings view.
Title of a menu item that resets all filter selections.
A label displayed above a chart that shows the memory usage of a process.
Section header for the resource usage section in the process details view.
A label displayed next to the run time of a process.
Label for the sandbox status of a process.
A button that saves the current color setting.
Placeholder text in the search bar.
A placeholder for a search field that shows the names of the currently selected filters.
A placeholder for a search bar that shows the number of filters being used.
Title of a menu item that appears in the menu that appears when you click the magnifying glass.
A label for the search query format.
Section header for the security information in the process details view.

trans-unit.id: Select process with a mouse click (Will reset text search)
trans-unit.source: Select process with a mouse click (Will reset text search)
trans-unit.target: Select process with a mouse click (Will reset text search)
trans-unit.note: A tooltip for the "Find process" button.
A column in the log table showing the sender of a log message.
A label for the user to enter a shortcut name.

trans-unit.id: Shortcut with the chosen name will be triggered on a chosen event with the following parameters: input=text&text=[process name]. ProcessSpy doesn't verify whether the Shortcut exists.
trans-unit.source: Shortcut with the chosen name will be triggered on a chosen event with the following parameters: input=text&text=[process name]. ProcessSpy doesn't verify whether the Shortcut exists.
trans-unit.target: Shortcut with the chosen name will be triggered on a chosen event with the following parameters: input=text&text=[process name]. ProcessSpy doesn't verify whether the Shortcut exists.
trans-unit.note: A description of the notification settings.
A heading displayed above the list of user's notification settings.
A toggle that shows or hides the CPU usage widget in the menu bar.
A checkbox to show the disk throughput usage in the menu bar.
A toggle to show the GPU usage in the menu bar.
A toggle to show the memory usage in the menu bar.

trans-unit.id: Show NEW badge for newly discovered processes
trans-unit.source: Show NEW badge for newly discovered processes
trans-unit.target: Show NEW badge for newly discovered processes
trans-unit.note: A toggle that shows a badge for newly discovered processes.
A label that describes the picker for selecting the app behavior.

trans-unit.id: Show aggregate values in hierarchy mode
trans-unit.source: Show aggregate values in hierarchy mode
trans-unit.target: Show aggregate values in hierarchy mode
trans-unit.note: A toggle that shows or hides aggregate values in the hierarchy mode.

trans-unit.id: Show confirmation dialog before sending signal
trans-unit.source: Show confirmation dialog before sending signal
trans-unit.target: Show confirmation dialog before sending signal
trans-unit.note: A toggle that lets the user enable or disable the confirmation dialog before sending a signal to a process.

trans-unit.id: Show dotted guides in hierarchy mode
trans-unit.source: Show dotted guides in hierarchy mode
trans-unit.target: Show dotted guides in hierarchy mode
trans-unit.note: A toggle that enables or disables dotted guides in hierarchy mode.

trans-unit.id: Show mini cpu graph next to each process name
trans-unit.source: Show mini cpu graph next to each process name
trans-unit.target: Show mini cpu graph next to each process name
trans-unit.note: A toggle that enables or disables the display of a mini CPU graph next to each process name.

trans-unit.id: Show secondary line in Name cell:
trans-unit.source: Show secondary line in Name cell:
trans-unit.target: Show secondary line in Name cell:
trans-unit.note: A label for a picker that lets the user choose whether to show a secondary line in the name of a process.

trans-unit.id: Show timeline next to each process name
trans-unit.source: Show timeline next to each process name
trans-unit.target: Show timeline next to each process name
trans-unit.note: A toggle that lets the user enable or disable the timeline view.
Column name for the "Signing Organization" column in the Process Spy app.
Display names for the popover height presets.
A label displayed in the sidebar tab for the list of open sockets.

trans-unit.id: Some options are omitted in free version.
trans-unit.source: Some options are omitted in free version.
trans-unit.target: Some options are omitted in free version.
trans-unit.note: A description of some options that are not available in the free version.
A label for the source of a socket.
A button that starts recording a process's history.
Label for the start time of a process.
The startup type of the process.
A column in the Process Spy app that shows the type of startup entry for a process.
A button that stops streaming logs.
A label for the subsystem column in the log stream.
A label for the subsystem filter picker.
A contact email for support.
A toggle that enables or disables the display of sustained high CPU usage alerts.
Display name for the "System" appearance mode.

A tooltip that shows the system CPU time and the percentage of the total CPU time that the system CPU time represents. The first argument is the string “0s”, the string “%1$argh %2$argm %3$args”, the string “%1$argm %2$args”, or the string “%args”. The second argument is the string “0%”, the string “<0.1%”, the string “%.1f%%”, or the string “%.0f%%”.
A toggle that enables or disables the background colors of the rows in a table.
The display name for the "Tall" popover height.

trans-unit.id: The string must contain placeholder for process name: [processName]
trans-unit.source: The string must contain placeholder for process name: [processName]
trans-unit.target: The string must contain placeholder for process name: [processName]
trans-unit.note: A description of the format of the search query.
A label for the theme picker.
A label displayed under the number of threads of a selected process.
A label displayed above a slider that lets the user set a CPU usage threshold.
A label displayed under the CPU usage percentage.
A label displayed above the process timeline.
A button that toggles the visibility of the inspector.
A label displayed next to the shortcut name.
A letter of the CPU.

trans-unit.id: UP: Time since last boot AWK: Time awake (excluding sleep)
trans-unit.source: UP: Time since last boot AWK: Time awake (excluding sleep)
trans-unit.target: UP: Time since last boot AWK: Time awake (excluding sleep)
trans-unit.note: A tooltip for the uptime block.
A placeholder text for a tab that is not yet implemented.
Label for the user initiated QoS class.
Label for the user interactive QoS class.
A label displayed above the user/system CPU time bar.

A tooltip that shows the user CPU time and the percentage of the total CPU time. The first argument is the string “0s”, the string “%1$argh %2$argm %3$args”, the string “%1$argm %2$args”, or the string “%args”. The second argument is the string “0%”, the string “<0.1%”, the string “%.1f%%”, or the string “%.0f%%”.
Label for the utility QoS class.
A placeholder for the button when the user is trying to apply a license key.
A label that links to the release notes for the app. The argument is the string “CFBundleName”, the string “CFBundleShortVersionString”, or the string “⚠️”.
A label for the view type picker.

trans-unit.id: We are transitioning to a more secure activation system. Please paste your Gumroad license key below to seamlessly migrate your account. Legacy activations will be phased out in an upcoming release. If you need help finding your key, contact processspy@icloud.com.
trans-unit.source: We are transitioning to a more secure activation system. Please paste your Gumroad license key below to seamlessly migrate your account. Legacy activations will be phased out in an upcoming release. If you need help finding your key, contact processspy@icloud.com.
trans-unit.target: We are transitioning to a more secure activation system. Please paste your Gumroad license key below to seamlessly migrate your account. Legacy activations will be phased out in an upcoming release. If you need help finding your key, contact processspy@icloud.com.
trans-unit.note: A description of the migration process.
A label for the height of the menu bar popover.
Label for the written bytes in an IO statistics row.
Yes
A human-readable string representation of a file size in bytes.
A link to the MIT license for the CGEventSupervisor open-source project.
A link to the MIT license for the CodeEditor project.
A link to the documentation.
A link to report a bug.
A link to request a feature.
A link to the Sparkle project.
A link to the support email.
A link to the MIT license for the swiftui-introspect package.
A label displayed in a row of a notification setting.
A label that indicates the event on which a notification should be triggered.
A period.
